title: seriphidium maritimum var. palmatum
intro: Seriphidium maritimum var. palmatum, also known as Sea Wormwood, is a variant of the Seriphidium maritimum plant, a shrub-like perennial that grows in coastal areas. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to the presence of essential oils such as camphor and borneol. The health benefits of Sea Wormwood include its ability to aid in the treatment of digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. In herbal preparations, Sea Wormwood is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be cautioned due to its potential toxicity and interactions with certain medications.
